title: agrostemma saxatile
intro: Agrostemma saxatile, also known as Corn Cockle, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pink or white flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Agrostemma saxatile include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Agrostemma saxatile can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
